name: golang-github-xanzy-go-cloudstack-dev
title: golang-github-xanzy-go-cloudstack-dev
summary: Golang API client to interact with Apache CloudStack
description:
Golang API client to interact with Apache CloudStack
This package covers the complete CloudStack API enabling Go programs to
interact with CloudStack in a simple and uniform way.
.
The cloudstack package is generated against the latest stable
CloudStack release, currently v4.8.x. The API doesn't change that
much, and where it does backwards compatibility between the old
and new versions is attempted.
.
Apache CLoudStack is an open source cloud computing software for
creating, managing, and deploying infrastructure cloud services. It
uses existing hypervisors such as KVM, VMware vSphere, and
XenServer/XCP for virtualization. In addition to its own API,
CloudStack also supports the Amazon Web Services (AWS) API and the
Open Cloud Computing Interface from the Open Grid Forum.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-xanzy-go-cloudstack-dev because Golang API client to interact with Apache CloudStack.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-xanzy-go-cloudstack-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-xanzy-go-cloudstack-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-xanzy-go-cloudstack-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
